segments.text: Then Zophar the Naamathite answered,
Therefore my thoughts answer me, even by reason of my haste that is in me.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: I have heard the reproof which puts me to shame. The spirit of my understanding answers me.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: Don’t you know this from old time, since man was placed on earth,
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: that the triumphing of the wicked is short, the joy of the godless but for a moment?
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: Though his height mount up to the heavens, and his head reach to the clouds,
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: yet he will perish forever like his own dung. Those who have seen him will say, ‘Where is he?’
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: He will fly away as a dream, and will not be found. Yes, he will be chased away like a vision of the night.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: The eye which saw him will see him no more, neither will his place see him any more.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: His children will seek the favor of the poor. His hands will give back his wealth.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: His bones are full of his youth, but youth will lie down with him in the dust.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: “Though wickedness is sweet in his mouth, though he hide it under his tongue,
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: though he spare it, and will not let it go, but keep it still within his mouth,
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: yet his food in his bowels is turned. It is cobra venom within him.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: He has swallowed down riches, and he will vomit them up again. God will cast them out of his belly.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: He will suck cobra venom. The viper’s tongue will kill him.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: He will not look at the rivers, the flowing streams of honey and butter.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: He will restore that for which he labored, and will not swallow it down. He will not rejoice according to the substance that he has gotten.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: For he has oppressed and forsaken the poor. He has violently taken away a house, and he will not build it up.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: “Because he knew no quietness within him, he will not save anything of that in which he delights.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: There was nothing left that he didn’t devour, therefore his prosperity will not endure.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: In the fullness of his sufficiency, distress will overtake him. The hand of everyone who is in misery will come on him.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: When he is about to fill his belly, God will cast the fierceness of his wrath on him. It will rain on him while he is eating.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: He will flee from the iron weapon. The bronze arrow will strike him through.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text:
He draws it out, and it comes out of his body. Yes, the glittering point comes out of his liver. 
Terrors are on him.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text:
All darkness is laid up for his treasures. An unfanned fire will devour him. 
It will consume that which is left in his tent.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: The heavens will reveal his iniquity. The earth will rise up against him.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: The increase of his house will depart. They will rush away in the day of his wrath.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: This is the portion of a wicked man from God, the heritage appointed to him by God.
segments.speaker: Zophar
